message.content:
Monitor the 12-experiment run at /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/run_all_12.log

This runs 12 experiments (4 models × 3 modes) serially with MAX_UPDATES=5000 (~30 min each, ~6 hours total).

Every 2 minutes, check:
1. grep -E "\[.*\/12\]|Result:|FAILED|SUCCESS|PASS=|ALL 12" /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/run_all_12.log
2. For the currently running experiment, check its individual log in /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/ — use `grep -oP '\d+/50000000' <log> | tail -1` to see step progress (divide by 2 for actual training steps)
3. Check for any errors: grep "RuntimeError\|Parent directory\|SIGKILL" in individual logs

Do NOT use `wait`. Use `sleep 120` between checks.

When the final summary appears ("Results: PASS=..., FAIL=..."), report back immediately with:
- How many passed/failed
- Which ones failed and why
- Total wall time

content.text: I'll start monitoring the 12-experiment run. Let me first check the current state of the log file.

input.description: Check main log for experiment status
List log files sorted by modification time

content.text: The experiment is still in the AutoDist planning/compilation phase. Training hasn't started yet. Now let me set up the periodic monitoring loop. I'll sleep 120 seconds between checks.

input.command: sleep 120 && echo "=== CHECK 1 $(date) ===" && echo "--- Main log ---" && grep -E "\[.*\/12\]|Result:|FAILED|SUCCESS|PASS=|ALL 12" /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/run_all_12.log | tail -20 && echo "--- Log files ---" && ls -lt /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/*.log 2>/dev/null | head -5 && echo "--- Current experiment progress ---" && for f in /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/*.log; do [ "$f" != "/home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/run_all_12.log" ] && echo "  $f:" && grep -oP '\d+/50000000' "$f" 2>/dev/null | tail -1 && grep -E "step|loss=|updates=" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tail -1; done && echo "--- Error check ---" && for f in /home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/*.log; do [ "$f" != "/home/yingbohao/llm-train/logs/run_all_12.log" ] && grep "RuntimeError\|Parent directory\|SIGKILL" "$f" 2>/dev/null | tail -2; done
